claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child is 20, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is 20, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care

Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I actually have two children I'd like to ask about. Alex is 8 years old, and Blake is 20. Should I give you information about both of them, or do we need to go through them one at a time?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thanks for letting me know about both of your children! I can gather information about both Alex and Blake, and then assess their eligibility together.

Based on the ages you've provided, I can already give you some initial guidance:

- **Alex (8 years old)**: Since Alex is under 16, they fall into the automatic qualification age category. I'll need to ask you a few more questions to complete the assessment.

- **Blake (20 years old)**: Unfortunately, children who are 20 or over are not eligible for Child Benefit, regardless of their circumstances.

Let me continue with the assessment for **Alex**. Since Alex is under 16, I need to check one thing:

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex (8 years old) working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

For an 8-year-old, I would expect the answer is that Alex is not in paid work, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - he's only 8! He's in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense for an 8-year-old. Let me continue with the assessment for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in his own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed by Alex himself, rather than benefits you receive for him.

For an 8-year-old, this would typically be "no" – he wouldn't be receiving such benefits. Can you confirm Alex is not receiving any benefits in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, Alex doesn't get any benefits in his own name. He's just a child.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex in an apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is 8 years old and in primary school, I assume he is **not** in any apprenticeship. Can you confirm that's correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's just in primary school like I said.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, just confirming for the record! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You only need to meet **ONE** of these:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You are a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, option 1 (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm living abroad currently.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. That's an important detail.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?**

Crown servants include:
- Members of the armed forces serving overseas
- Civil servants posted abroad
- Diplomatic staff

Or are you living in certain qualifying countries for other official purposes?

If not, could you tell me a bit more about your situation? For example:
- Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?
- Are you temporarily abroad but normally resident in the UK?
- What is your connection to the UK?

This will help me determine if you meet the residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I just live abroad now - I don't have any of those special statuses you mentioned. I'm not normally resident in the UK either.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being clear about your situation. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, I need to record that you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I now have enough information to provide you with the eligibility outcome for both of your children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment - Final Outcome

I'm sorry to say that based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for either of your children**.

---

### Alex (age 8) - ❌ Not Eligible

**Reason:** Claimant does not meet UK residency requirements

Alex himself would qualify as an eligible child – he's under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship. However, **you** (the claimant) must meet residency requirements to claim Child Benefit, and you've confirmed that you:
- Don't normally live in the UK
- Are not a Crown servant
- Don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme

---

### Blake (age 20) - ❌ Not Eligible

**Reason:** Child is over the age limit

Children who are 20 years old or over cannot qualify for Child Benefit under any circumstances.

---

### What You Can Do

If your circumstances change in the future, you may become eligible:

- **If you return to live in the UK** and normally reside here, you could claim for Alex (as long as he remains under 16, or up to 20 if in approved education/training)
- **If you work abroad as a Crown servant**, you may also qualify

For more information, you can visit: [GOV.UK Child Benefit Eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I understand this may not be the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help clarify?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship - so he would normally qualify as a 'qualifying child'. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements. The claimant confirmed they do not normally live in the UK, are not a Crown servant, and do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. Children aged 20 or over are not eligible for Child Benefit under any circumstances, regardless of the claimant's residency status. This is an automatic disqualification based on age.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for either child. Blake (age 20) is too old to qualify under any circumstances. While Alex (age 8) would otherwise be a qualifying child, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements - they confirmed they live abroad, are not normally resident in the UK, are not a Crown servant, and do not have any qualifying immigration status under the EU Settlement Scheme.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements. Therefore, Child Benefit cannot be claimed for Alex.

Blake is 20 years old. Children aged 20 or over are not eligible for Child Benefit under any circumstances, regardless of the claimant's residency status. This is an automatic disqualification based on age.
